zh: 態度，對某事的看法或感受
en: His attitude towards learning English helped him improve quickly.
en_example: His attitude towards learning English helped him improve quickly.
zh_example: His attitude towards learning English helped him improve quickly.
ja: 態度、物事に対する考え方や感じ方
ja_example: His attitude towards learning English helped him improve quickly.

en: She can communicate well in English despite her low level.
en_example: She can communicate well in English despite her low level.
zh_example: She can communicate well in English despite her low level.
ja: コミュニケーションをとる、意思を伝える
ja_example: She can communicate well in English despite her low level.

en: She dreaded going to piano lessons because she was afraid of making mistakes.
en_example: She dreaded going to piano lessons because she was afraid of making mistakes.
zh_example: She dreaded going to piano lessons because she was afraid of making mistakes.
ja_example: She dreaded going to piano lessons because she was afraid of making mistakes.

en: His self image affected his confidence in speaking English.
en_example: His self image affected his confidence in speaking English.
zh_example: His self image affected his confidence in speaking English.
ja: 自己イメージ、自分自身の認識
ja_example: His self image affected his confidence in speaking English.

zh: 網咖，提供上網服務的場所
en: I went to a cyber cafe when my computer broke down.
en_example: I went to a cyber cafe when my computer broke down.
zh_example: I went to a cyber cafe when my computer broke down.
ja: インターネットカフェ、ネット利用ができる場所
ja_example: I went to a cyber cafe when my computer broke down.

en: He showed no embarrassment even though his friends were watching him play.
en_example: He showed no embarrassment even though his friends were watching him play.
zh_example: He showed no embarrassment even though his friends were watching him play.
ja_example: He showed no embarrassment even though his friends were watching him play.

en: She was focused on the conversation, not on her mistakes.
en_example: She was focused on the conversation, not on her mistakes.
zh_example: She was focused on the conversation, not on her mistakes.
ja: 集中している、注意を向けている
ja_example: She was focused on the conversation, not on her mistakes.

zh: 有效的，產生預期效果的
en: Being focused on the person you speak to makes communication more effective.
en_example: Being focused on the person you speak to makes communication more effective.
zh_example: Being focused on the person you speak to makes communication more effective.
ja: 効果的な、期待した効果をもたらす
ja_example: Being focused on the person you speak to makes communication more effective.

en: Native speakers usually have a natural fluency in their language.
en_example: Native speakers usually have a natural fluency in their language.
zh_example: Native speakers usually have a natural fluency in their language.
ja_example: Native speakers usually have a natural fluency in their language.

en: Most English conversations in the world involve non-native speakers.
en_example: Most English conversations in the world involve non-native speakers.
zh_example: Most English conversations in the world involve non-native speakers.
ja_example: Most English conversations in the world involve non-native speakers.

en: English is a tool to get a result, not just an art to master.
en_example: English is a tool to get a result, not just an art to master.
zh_example: English is a tool to get a result, not just an art to master.
ja: 道具、目的を達成するための手段
ja_example: English is a tool to get a result, not just an art to master.

en: Clarity in communication is more important than perfect grammar.
en_example: Clarity in communication is more important than perfect grammar.
zh_example: Clarity in communication is more important than perfect grammar.
ja: 明瞭さ、はっきりしていること
ja_example: Clarity in communication is more important than perfect grammar.

zh: 多工處理，同時處理多項任務
en: Your brain cannot multitask well when you try to speak and correct yourself at the same time.
en_example: Your brain cannot multitask well when you try to speak and correct yourself at the same time.
zh_example: Your brain cannot multitask well when you try to speak and correct yourself at the same time.
ja: マルチタスク、複数の作業を同時に行うこと
ja_example: Your brain cannot multitask well when you try to speak and correct yourself at the same time.

zh: 自信，對自己能力的信任
en: She spoke English with confidence despite her mistakes.
en_example: She spoke English with confidence despite her mistakes.
zh_example: She spoke English with confidence despite her mistakes.
ja: 自信、自分の能力を信じること
ja_example: She spoke English with confidence despite her mistakes.

en: Focus on the result you want to achieve in communication.
en_example: Focus on the result you want to achieve in communication.
zh_example: Focus on the result you want to achieve in communication.
ja_example: Focus on the result you want to achieve in communication.
